description: Design System component catalog — maps visual needs to registered DS primitives. Read by storyboard agent (to pick components) and production agent (to resolve implementations).

components.name: Stagger Text Reveal
useCases: Text appearing on screen for the first time (title cards, section headers)
Dramatic character-by-character or word-by-word text entrance
Quote or statement reveal with emphasis
components.whenToUse: A piece of text needs to appear on screen with animation. Each character or word springs in sequentially.
components.whenNotToUse: Text that needs to CHANGE to different text (use text-rotate instead). Text that should just fade in without per-character animation (use blur-fade-in).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'stagger-text-reveal'. Configure with visual.motionConfig.splitBy ('characters' | 'words') and visual.motionConfig.staggerFrom ('first' | 'last' | 'center').
components.exampleScenario: Hook scene title 'The $40 Billion Question' appears word by word from center outward.

components.name: Text Rotate
useCases: Cycling through multiple values in the same position (e.g., '$100 → $200 → $500')
Before/after comparisons where one word changes ('costs $X' → 'costs $Y')
Showing evolution or progression through text replacement
Countdown or list items rotating in place
components.whenToUse: Multiple text values need to appear sequentially in the SAME position. The text exits, container resizes, new text enters — a 3-phase cycle.
components.whenNotToUse: Text appearing once and staying (use stagger-text-reveal). A number counting up smoothly (use counter-up). Two texts shown side by side (use comparison layout, not rotation).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'text-rotate'. Provide visual.motionConfig.texts = ['value1', 'value2', ...] for the rotation sequence.
components.exampleScenario: Scene shows 'Average salary: $X' where X rotates through '$52K → $65K → $78K' to show progression over decades.

components.name: Container Text Flip
useCases: Word cycling with blur-fade transition (softer than slide)
Hero text with rotating keywords and blur entrance
Container that auto-sizes with a pill background
components.whenToUse: Multiple words need to cycle in place with a soft blur-fade transition. Softer feel than text-rotate's slide animation. Optional pill/badge background.
components.whenNotToUse: When you need dramatic slide-in motion (use text-rotate). Single text entrance (use stagger-text-reveal). Just blur fade without cycling (use blur-fade-in).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'container-text-flip'. Provide visual.motionConfig.texts = ['word1', 'word2', ...]. Optional visual.motionConfig.showPill = true for badge style.
components.exampleScenario: Hero scene: 'Make your code [better/modern/beautiful/awesome]' with words cycling in a soft blur-fade inside a gradient pill.

components.name: Counter Up
useCases: Numeric value counting up from 0 to target (e.g., '$0 → $1,234,567')
Percentage animations (0% → 85%)
Statistics reveal with smooth number interpolation
components.whenToUse: A single numeric value needs to animate from one number to another smoothly. The number itself is the visual focus.
components.whenNotToUse: Text changing to different text (use text-rotate). Multiple numbers shown as a list (use stagger-text-reveal for the list, counter-up for individual numbers).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'counter-up'. Provide visual.motionConfig.from and visual.motionConfig.to numbers, optional visual.motionConfig.prefix ('$') and visual.motionConfig.suffix ('%').
components.exampleScenario: Big stat scene: '$0' counts up to '$2.4 Trillion' with a prefix dollar sign.

components.name: Bar Grow
useCases: Horizontal or vertical bars animating from 0 to their target width/height
Progress bar filling up
Bar chart bars growing in
components.whenToUse: A rectangular bar shape needs to animate from empty to its target size. Used inside data visualizations or as standalone progress indicators.
components.whenNotToUse: Pie/donut charts (different primitive). Line charts (different primitive). Just a number changing (use counter-up).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'bar-grow'. Provide visual.motionConfig.direction ('horizontal' | 'vertical').
components.exampleScenario: Comparison bars for 'US vs EU spending' grow from left to right, staggered.

components.name: Blur Fade In
useCases: Subtle content entrance — blurred and transparent → sharp and opaque
Background element appearing without drawing too much attention
Secondary text or labels fading in
components.whenToUse: Content should appear subtly, going from blurry+transparent to clear. Less dramatic than stagger-text-reveal.
components.whenNotToUse: Hero text that needs dramatic entrance (use stagger-text-reveal). Text that cycles through values (use text-rotate).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'blur-fade-in'.
components.exampleScenario: Source citation text fades in at bottom of screen during a data scene.

components.name: Dot Grid
useCases: Data-focused or analytical scenes — gives a technical, clean feel
Explainer scenes where the visual is mostly text/charts
components.whenToUse: Scene has a data/analytical tone. Works well behind charts, metrics, or comparison layouts.
components.whenNotToUse: Cinematic/emotional scenes (use aurora or particles). Scenes with busy stock footage behind (atmosphere competes with video).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = 'dot-grid'.
components.exampleScenario: Background for a bar chart comparison scene — subtle animated dots behind the chart.

components.name: Film Grain
useCases: Cinematic or documentary-style scenes
Historical content — adds a vintage/archival feel
Narrative-heavy scenes where mood matters
components.whenToUse: Scene needs a cinematic, textured feel. Great for narrative sections, historical context, or emotional moments.
components.whenNotToUse: Clean data visualization scenes (use dot-grid). Bright/modern/techy aesthetic (use particles).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = 'film-grain'.
components.exampleScenario: Historical context scene about 1970s economic policy — film grain adds era-appropriate texture.

components.name: Glass Surface
useCases: Cards floating over a background — glassmorphism effect
Data cards, stat boxes, comparison panels
Any container that should feel modern and semi-transparent
components.whenToUse: A container/card needs to feel like frosted glass floating over the background. Content inside should be readable against the blurred backdrop.
components.whenNotToUse: Fully opaque cards with no transparency (use flat). Cards that need to glow/pulse for emphasis (use glow).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.surface = 'glass'.
components.exampleScenario: Stat card showing '$2.4T' floats over an aurora atmosphere with glassmorphic background.

components.name: Flat Surface
useCases: Clean, minimal card with solid or near-solid background
Data tables, comparison grids
When background atmosphere should not show through the card
components.whenToUse: Container should be clean and opaque — content readability is the priority, not visual flair.
components.whenNotToUse: When background atmosphere should show through (use glass or frosted).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.surface = 'flat'.
components.exampleScenario: Comparison table with two columns — solid dark cards with white text, no transparency.

components.name: Glow Surface
useCases: Hero stat or key number that needs maximum visual emphasis
Call-to-action cards
Highlight cards in a grid (one card glows, others are flat)
components.whenToUse: A card/container needs to demand attention — animated glow border that pulses or shifts color.
components.whenNotToUse: Subtle or secondary content (use glass or flat). Many cards in a grid (glow on ALL cards is visual noise — use glow selectively).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.surface = 'glow'.
components.exampleScenario: Hero stat '$2.4 Trillion' in a glowing card that pulses — the single most important number in the video.

components.name: Bar Chart
useCases: Comparing quantities across discrete categories (e.g. GDP by country)
Ranked data with visual bar lengths
Side-by-side category comparison with staggered animation
components.whenToUse: When comparing discrete categories by a numeric value. Supports both horizontal (default) and vertical orientation.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing trends over time (use line-chart). When showing proportions of a whole (use pie-chart). When comparing exactly 2 entities head-to-head (use comparison-table).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'bar-chart'. Provide items: [{label, value}]. Optional: orientation = 'vertical' | 'horizontal', colors array, unit prefix.
components.exampleScenario: 5 countries' average salaries as horizontal bars, growing left to right with staggered springs.

components.name: Horizontal Bar Chart
useCases: Country-level salary or GDP comparisons with labeled bars
Gradient-colored ranking bars (green-to-red spectrum)
Re-sort animation showing ranking changes between metrics
components.whenToUse: When you need dedicated horizontal bars with gradient coloring, displayValue formatting, and optional re-sort animation. More specialized than BarChart.
components.whenNotToUse: Simple bar comparisons without gradient or re-sort (use bar-chart). Vertical bars (use bar-chart with orientation='vertical').
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'horizontal-bar-chart'. Provide bars: [{label, code, value, displayValue}]. Optional: gradientColors, animation.
components.exampleScenario: 10 countries ranked by nominal salary with green→red gradient bars, then re-sorting by PPP-adjusted values.

components.name: Line Chart
useCases: Showing trends over time (e.g. inflation rate 2000–2025)
Continuous data with labeled X-axis points
Area-under-curve visualization with subtle fill
components.whenToUse: When showing continuous data trends over time. Line draws progressively with spring physics, points appear staggered.
components.whenNotToUse: When comparing discrete categories (use bar-chart). When showing proportions (use pie-chart). When needing multi-series racing animation (use horse-race-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'line-chart'. Provide items: [{label, value}] as time-ordered points. Optional: colors[0] for line color, unit.
components.exampleScenario: CPI inflation trend 2000–2025 drawn as a line with subtle area fill underneath.

components.name: Pie Chart
useCases: Showing proportions of a whole (e.g. budget allocation)
Donut chart with animated segment sweep
Category distribution with legend
components.whenToUse: When showing how parts make up a whole. Segments sweep in clockwise with staggered spring animation. Renders as donut (55% inner radius).
components.whenNotToUse: When comparing absolute values (use bar-chart). When there are more than 8 categories (too many slices). When showing time-based data (use line-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'pie-chart'. Provide items: [{label, value}]. Optional: colors array, title.
components.exampleScenario: Government spending breakdown: Defense 25%, Healthcare 30%, Education 20%, Other 25% — donut with legend.

components.name: Counter
useCases: Hero stat reveal with animated counting (e.g. '$2,400,000')
Big number with optional breakdown bars below
KPI display with contextual title and accent line
components.whenToUse: When a single large number is the visual focus. Counts from 0 to target with spring physics. Optional breakdown bars show composition.
components.whenNotToUse: When multiple metrics need equal weight (use comparison-table). When the number should cycle through values (use text-rotate).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'counter'. Provide counterValue, counterPrefix ('$'), counterSuffix ('%'). Optional: items for breakdown bars.
components.exampleScenario: Hero scene: '$2.4 Trillion' counts up from 0 with a pulse effect, followed by 3 breakdown bars.

components.name: Comparison Table
useCases: Head-to-head entity comparison (2 items: tug-of-war bar)
Multi-metric duel comparison (butterfly chart with paired bars)
Multi-item ranked comparison (3+ items: horizontal bar list)
components.whenToUse: When comparing entities. Auto-selects mode: 2 items = tug-of-war, 3+ items = horizontal bars, duel config = butterfly chart.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing time trends (use line-chart). When showing proportions (use pie-chart). Simple category bars without comparison context (use bar-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'comparison-table'. For duel mode: provide duel: {left, right} + paired items. For tug-of-war: 2 items. For list: 3+ items.
components.exampleScenario: US vs EU butterfly chart comparing GDP, Population, Military spending — bars grow from center outward.

components.name: Timeline Chart
useCases: Chronological events along a horizontal axis
Historical milestones with values at each marker
Policy timeline with alternating top/bottom labels
components.whenToUse: When showing events or values at specific points in time on a horizontal axis. Markers alternate above/below the line with staggered entrance.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing continuous data trends (use line-chart). When comparing categories without time (use bar-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'timeline'. Provide items: [{label, value}] in chronological order. Optional: unit, colors.
components.exampleScenario: Key economic policy events 1970–2020 — markers along a timeline with values showing impact at each event.

components.name: Scale Comparison
useCases: Visual size/magnitude comparison using proportional circles
Dramatic 'drop vs gallon' or 'Earth vs Jupiter' comparisons
Making abstract numbers tangible through area-proportional shapes
components.whenToUse: When the ratio between values is the key insight and circles make the difference visceral. Uses area-proportional (sqrt) sizing.
components.whenNotToUse: When exact values matter more than visual impact (use bar-chart). When there are many items (use bar-chart). When items are close in value (circles look similar).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'scale-comparison'. Provide items: [{label, value}]. Optional: colors, unit.
components.exampleScenario: US military budget ($800B) vs education budget ($80B) — two circles showing 10x scale difference.

components.name: Progress Ring
useCases: Percentage completion indicator (e.g. '85% of budget spent')
Single KPI displayed as a circular gauge
Goal progress visualization
components.whenToUse: When showing a single percentage/progress value as a circular indicator. Ring fills with spring animation, counter in center.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing multiple categories (use pie-chart). When the value is not percentage-based (use counter). When comparing multiple metrics (use bar-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'progress-ring'. Provide counterValue (0-100), counterSuffix ('%'). Optional: colors[0] for ring color.
components.exampleScenario: Employee satisfaction score: ring fills to 78% with the number counting up in the center.

components.name: Quadrant Scatter
useCases: 2D scatter plot with four labeled quadrants (e.g. Dream/Grind/Chill/Trap)
Multi-country comparison on two axes with camera zoom
Spotlight/tooltip overlays on specific data points
Progressive reveal of dots then labels
components.whenToUse: When two dimensions define four meaningful quadrants and data points need to be plotted with rich interactivity (zoom, tooltips, connectors, annotations).
components.whenNotToUse: When data is 1-dimensional (use bar-chart). When there are fewer than 5 data points (overkill — use comparison-table). When axes don't form meaningful quadrants.
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'quadrant-scatter'. Provide points: [{label, x, y, quadrant}], xAxis, yAxis configs. Optional: cameraZoom, spotlights, tooltips, connectorLines.
components.exampleScenario: 33 OECD countries plotted on Hours Worked vs Hourly Wage — camera zooms into 'Dream' quadrant to spotlight Nordic countries.

components.name: Horse Race Chart
useCases: Multi-series time-lapse animation (e.g. asset prices racing over decades)
Animated year counter with sliding window X-axis
Event annotations as notification-stack overlays
Shrinkflation markers on the timeline
components.whenToUse: When multiple time series need to 'race' against each other with a year counter. Supports log scale, Catmull-Rom splines, label collision avoidance, and event annotation stack.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing a single trend line (use line-chart). When data is not time-based (use bar-chart). When scene is under 5 seconds (too complex for short scenes).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'horse-race-chart'. Provide series: [{id, label, color, data: [{date, ratio}]}], timeRange, sceneYearRanges. Optional: annotations, shrinkflationMarkers, logScale.
components.exampleScenario: Gold vs S&P 500 vs Oil racing from 1970–2025 with sliding window, log scale, and crisis event annotations popping in.

components.name: Calendar Grid
useCases: Visualizing working days in a year with highlighted portion
Calendar heatmap showing extra effort days
Country comparison card alongside calendar visualization
components.whenToUse: When showing a proportion of days in a year visually. 12-month grid with highlighted cells that build progressively. Optional comparison card sidebar.
components.whenNotToUse: When comparing values numerically (use bar-chart). When showing time-series trends (use line-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'calendar-grid'. Provide months, highlightedDays, highlightColor. Optional: labels, comparisonCard, connectorLabel.
components.exampleScenario: 365-day calendar where the last 55 days glow red — representing extra working days needed for purchasing power parity.

components.name: Hook Scene
useCases: Video opening hook with question text + clock icon
Multi-phase hook with text → stock footage hard cuts
Attention-grabbing intro with configurable phase timings
components.whenToUse: When the video needs a cinematic opening hook. Shows question text with animated clock, then hard-cuts through stock video clips.
components.whenNotToUse: When the hook needs data visualization (use hook-punchline). When the hook is purely text-based without footage (use title-card).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'hook-scene'. Provide questionText, slug, videos: {worldmap, whitecollar, factory}. Optional: phaseTimes for custom cuts.
components.exampleScenario: 'What is one hour of your life worth?' text with ticking clock → world map footage → white-collar worker → factory worker.

components.name: Title Card
useCases: Full-screen section title with subtitle
Concept introduction card (e.g. 'Purchasing Power Parity')
Minimalist centered text with decorative accent line
components.whenToUse: When a concept or section needs a clean, centered title reveal with optional subtitle and decorative line. Minimalist dark-cozy aesthetic.
components.whenNotToUse: When the title needs data visualization alongside it (use metric-scene). When it's a video hook (use hook-scene).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'title-card'. Provide title, subtitle. Optional: titleColor, subtitleColor, icon.
components.exampleScenario: 'Purchasing Power Parity (PPP)' title springs in, accent line draws, subtitle '~3,000 goods tracked by OECD' fades in below.

components.name: Metric Scene
useCases: Two-phase data scene: split comparison card → formula build
Before/after price comparison (e.g. 2000 vs 2025 wages)
Formula explanation with sequential term reveals
components.whenToUse: When showing a before/after comparison followed by a formula explanation. Phase 1: split cards with counter animations. Phase 2: formula parts appear sequentially.
components.whenNotToUse: When only showing a formula without comparison (use composite-phases). When just comparing two entities without formula (use comparison-table).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'metric-scene'. Provide phase1: {left: {year, wage, product, price}, right: {...}}, phase2: {formulaParts: [...]}.
components.exampleScenario: 2000 ($572/week, eggs $0.91) vs 2025 ($1,206/week, eggs $4.25) split card → 'Price Growth ÷ Wage Growth = RUPI' formula build.

components.name: Split Comparison
useCases: Side-by-side city/country comparison with itemized lists
Grocery basket comparison (same items, different prices)
Split-screen visual PPP explanation
components.whenToUse: When comparing two cities/countries with itemized price lists side by side. Items appear staggered with totals revealed at the end.
components.whenNotToUse: When comparing aggregate values (use comparison-table). When comparing more than 2 entities (use bar-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'split-comparison'. Provide left/right: {city, country, items: [{name, price}], total}.
components.exampleScenario: Zurich vs Mexico City grocery basket — same items (milk, bread, eggs) with dramatically different prices.

components.name: End Card Scene
useCases: Video outro with fade-to-black and optional final question
YouTube end screen with subscribe/next-video placeholders
Channel watermark display
components.whenToUse: When the video needs a final end card with YouTube end screen elements. Supports fade-to-black, final question text, and configurable end screen placements.
components.whenNotToUse: When the closing needs narrative text sequences (use closing-sequence). When closing involves chart + video + end card (use closing-scene).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'end-card'. Provide fadeToBlack: true, channelName. Optional: finalQuestion, youtubeEndScreen config.
components.exampleScenario: Final question 'What is your hour really worth?' fades in, holds, then fades to black with YouTube end screen overlay zone.

components.name: Closing Scene
useCases: Multi-phase closing: chart spotlight → stock video → end card
Cinematic video ending with crossfade transitions
Chart-to-footage-to-endscreen pipeline
components.whenToUse: When the video closing needs three sequential phases: data chart, stock footage, and end card — all with crossfade transitions.
components.whenNotToUse: When closing is simple text + fade (use closing-sequence or end-card-scene). When no stock footage is needed.
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'closing-scene'. Provide chartConfig, chartEndSec, videoSrc, videoStartSec, videoEndSec, endScreenStartSec, endScreenConfig.
components.exampleScenario: QuadrantScatter spotlight → earth-from-space footage → EndCardScene with subscribe overlay — smooth crossfades between phases.

components.name: Closing Sequence
useCases: Multi-step text sequence for video conclusion
Sequential text reveals with underline accents
YouTube end screen safe-zone aware closing
components.whenToUse: When the closing needs sequential text statements that build toward a final message. Text stays in upper 65% to avoid YouTube end screen zone.
components.whenNotToUse: When closing needs chart or stock footage (use closing-scene). When just a simple fade-to-black (use end-card-scene).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'closing-sequence'. Provide textSequence: [{text, weight?, size?, holdSec?, underlineColor?}]. Optional: channelName, showSubscribe.
components.exampleScenario: Step 1: 'It's not just about money.' → Step 2: 'It's about time.' → Step 3: 'Your time.' → Step 4: channel name + subscribe.

components.name: Hook Punchline
useCases: Data-driven hook with quick bar flash then blur-to-question
Pattern interrupt: bars appear briefly, then blur + reveal question
Provocative 'why doesn't it feel that way?' moment
components.whenToUse: When the hook needs a quick data flash followed by a provocative question. Bars flash (red above / green below baseline), blur out, then question text fades in.
components.whenNotToUse: When the hook is purely text + footage (use hook-scene). When the hook needs sustained data visualization (use bar-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'hook-punchline'. Provide questionText, aboveLine: ['Eggs', 'Coffee'], belowLine: ['Chips', 'Milk', ...].
components.exampleScenario: Quick flash of 6 bars — 2 red (above CPI), 4 green (below CPI) — blurs out → 'So why doesn't it feel that way?' fades in.

components.name: Composite Phases
useCases: Multi-phase scene combining different chart types sequentially
SplitComparison → TitleCard → HorizontalBarChart in one scene
Crossfade transitions between sub-scenes within a single scene
components.whenToUse: When a single scene needs to show multiple chart types in sequence with crossfade transitions. Phases get proportional duration within the scene.
components.whenNotToUse: When each visualization should be its own scene (split into separate scenes). When phases don't need crossfade (use Sequence directly).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'composite-phases'. Provide phases: [{type, durationSec, ...phaseSpecificConfig}].
components.exampleScenario: Scene 003: SplitComparison (15s grocery basket) → TitleCard (5s PPP concept) → HorizontalBarChart (remaining: PPP bars) — all with crossfades.

components.name: Salary Shuffle Scene
useCases: Animated ranking re-sort (salary → hourly rate) with position swaps
Country cards that physically move to new positions
Dramatic reveal of ranking changes when switching metrics
components.whenToUse: When country/entity rankings change dramatically between two metrics and the position-swap animation makes that visceral. Cards physically slide to new positions.
components.whenNotToUse: When rankings don't change (use bar-chart). When comparing only 2 entities (use comparison-table). When there's no re-sort moment (use horizontal-bar-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'salary-shuffle'. Provide countries: [{code, label, salary, hourlyRate}], initialSort, resortTo, resortLabel. Optional: resortTriggerFrame.
components.exampleScenario: 10 countries sorted by salary — at frame 360, cards shuffle to hourly rate order: Switzerland drops, Japan rises dramatically.

components.name: Ranking Resort Scene
useCases: Two-column ranked list comparison (e.g. nominal vs PPP rank)
Spotlight highlights showing dramatic rank changes
Country rank cards with positive/negative indicators
components.whenToUse: When showing how rankings differ between two metrics in a dual-column format. Each column has ranked items with optional positive/negative highlights.
components.whenNotToUse: When the re-sort should be animated as position swaps (use salary-shuffle-scene). When comparing only values without ranks (use bar-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'ranking-resort'. Provide leftColumn: {title, items: [{rank, label, value, highlight?}]}, rightColumn: {...}. Optional: spotlightDelay.
components.exampleScenario: Left: 'By Salary' (Lux #1, Swiss #2, US #3) vs Right: 'By $/Hour' (Lux #1, US #5, Swiss #7) — Swiss drops highlighted in red.

components.name: Deflator Summary Grid
useCases: 2x2 grid comparing multiple deflators (CPI, PCE, Gold, Housing)
Above/below baseline mini bar charts for each deflator
Summary overview showing which products are above/below inflation
components.whenToUse: When comparing multiple deflator/inflation metrics in a grid layout. Each quadrant shows above-baseline (red) and below-baseline (green) bars with staggered entrance.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing only one deflator's data (use bar-chart). When comparing deflators over time (use horse-race-chart).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.dataChart.type = 'deflator-summary-grid'. Provide charts: [{deflator, above: [...], below: [...]}]. Optional: title, source.
components.exampleScenario: 2x2 grid: CPI-U, PCE, Housing Deflator, Gold — each showing which products are above (red) or below (green) their baseline.

components.name: Hook Reveal
useCases: Big number deflation reveal (e.g. '26,000%' shrinks to '132%')
Before/after number shock with dramatic size transition
Opening hook with context line and counting-ticker variant
components.whenToUse: When the hook reveals that a big number is misleading by deflating it dramatically. Supports classic (size-shrink) and counting-ticker variants.
components.whenNotToUse: When the hook doesn't involve number transformation (use hook-scene). When showing data bars (use hook-punchline).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.sceneType = 'hook-reveal'. Provide bigNumber, smallNumber. Optional: subtitle, contextLine, variant ('classic' | 'counting-ticker'), bigHoldDuration, deflationDuration.
components.exampleScenario: Context: 'The Dow Jones · Since 1925' → '26,000%' holds → deflates to '132%' → subtitle: 'in gold terms'.

components.name: Formula Card
useCases: Animated formula explanation (e.g. 'Asset Price ÷ Gold Price = Gold Ratio')
Sequential formula part reveals with example line
Educational formula breakdown for data explainer videos
components.whenToUse: When explaining a formula or calculation concept. Parts slide/fade in sequentially, then example line appears. Clean editorial style.
components.whenNotToUse: When the formula needs before/after data comparison (use metric-scene). When showing just a number (use counter).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.sceneType = 'formula-card'. Provide formulaParts: ['Asset Price', '÷', 'Gold Price', '=', 'Gold Ratio']. Optional: example, dataBadge.
components.exampleScenario: 'Asset Price' slides in → '÷' → 'Gold Price' → '=' → 'Gold Ratio' — then example: 'Oil $67 ÷ Gold $2,990 = 0.022 oz'.

components.name: Asset Parade
useCases: Sequential asset calculation showcase (formula → per-asset results)
Gold ratio calculations for multiple assets in sequence
Parade-style animation: each asset gets its own calculation moment
components.whenToUse: When showing the same formula applied to multiple assets sequentially. Formula shows first, then each asset parades through with its calculation. Light editorial theme.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing all assets simultaneously (use bar-chart or scoreboard). When formula isn't needed (use counter).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.sceneType = 'asset-parade'. Provide assets: [{name, price, ratio, color}], goldPrice, formulaParts. Optional: formulaDuration, assetDuration.
components.exampleScenario: Formula: 'Price ÷ Gold = Ratio' → Oil $67/0.022oz → Dow $42K/14.0oz → Housing $420K/140.5oz — each asset slides in for 3.5s.

components.name: Scoreboard
useCases: Ranked bar chart with editorial light theme
Final results scoreboard with staggered bar entrance
Asset performance summary with period labels
components.whenToUse: When showing final ranked results in a clean editorial style. Bars stagger in from top to bottom with spring animation. Light background theme.
components.whenNotToUse: When dark-cozy theme is required (use bar-chart). When showing time-series data (use horse-race-chart or line-chart).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.sceneType = 'scoreboard'. Provide items: [{label, value, color, suffix?, period?}]. Optional: title, footerText.
components.exampleScenario: Gold Ratio Scoreboard: Oil 0.022oz, S&P 14.0oz, Housing 140.5oz — bars staggered with period labels '1925-2025'.

components.name: Closing CTA
useCases: Clean editorial closing card with subscribe CTA
Light-theme video outro with end screen placeholders
Channel name reveal with separator line
components.whenToUse: When the video needs a light-themed closing card with subscribe CTA and YouTube end screen placeholder slots.
components.whenNotToUse: When dark-cozy theme is needed for closing (use closing-sequence or end-card-scene). When closing needs data visualization (use closing-scene).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.sceneType = 'closing-cta'. Optional: message, channelName, ctaText, showEndScreen.
components.exampleScenario: 'Nominal returns are an illusion.' message → 'The World With Numbers' channel name → Subscribe text + two video slot placeholders.

components.name: Scene Visual
useCases: Full-screen background image/video with Ken Burns effect
Stock footage or AI image as scene background
Text overlay on cinematic gradient when no image available
components.whenToUse: When a scene needs a full-screen image/video background with slow zoom+pan (Ken Burns). Falls back to cinematic gradient when no asset is available.
components.whenNotToUse: When the scene needs data charts (use any chart template). When the scene is purely text (use title-card).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.sceneType = 'scene-visual'. Provide visual: {type, assetPath}. For text-overlay: visual: {type: 'text-overlay', lines: [...]}.
components.exampleScenario: Stock photo of a city skyline with slow Ken Burns zoom, voiceover plays over it. Ranking badge in corner for composite scenes.

components.name: Particles
useCases: Futuristic or tech-focused scene backgrounds
Ambient floating particles for modern aesthetic
Abstract background for hero moments
components.whenToUse: Scene needs a modern, techy feel with floating particles. Good for hero stats and futuristic themes.
components.whenNotToUse: Data-heavy analytical scenes (use dot-grid). Cinematic/vintage scenes (use film-grain).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = 'particles'.
components.exampleScenario: Hero stat scene with floating particles behind a glowing counter card.

components.name: Aurora
useCases: Dramatic hero moments with flowing color gradients
Emotional or climactic scene backgrounds
Premium feel for key reveals
components.whenToUse: Scene needs maximum visual drama — flowing aurora-like gradients behind hero content.
components.whenNotToUse: Clean analytical scenes (use dot-grid). Subtle/understated moments (use film-grain).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = 'aurora'.
components.exampleScenario: Video's most important stat revealed with aurora gradients pulsing behind a glass surface card.

components.name: Flickering Grid
useCases: Tech/matrix aesthetic backgrounds
Data processing or computation visualization
Digital/cyber themed scenes
components.whenToUse: Scene needs a digital/matrix-like background with randomly flickering grid cells.
components.whenNotToUse: Organic or cinematic scenes (use film-grain). Clean analytical scenes (use dot-grid).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = 'flickering-grid'.
components.exampleScenario: Technology comparison scene with flickering grid background behind flat surface cards.

components.name: Slide Up
useCases: Content entering from below the frame
List items appearing one by one from bottom
Card or panel sliding up into position
components.whenToUse: Content should enter from below with spring physics. Clean, purposeful upward motion.
components.whenNotToUse: Text that needs per-character animation (use stagger-text-reveal). Content that should fade in without movement (use blur-fade-in).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'slide-up'.
components.exampleScenario: List of comparison items slide up one by one with staggered delays.

components.name: Scale In
useCases: Content scaling from 0 to full size
Emphasis entrance for important elements
Icon or badge pop-in effect
components.whenToUse: Element should scale from nothing to full size with spring bounce. Good for emphasis on important items.
components.whenNotToUse: Text entrance (use stagger-text-reveal). Subtle content appearance (use blur-fade-in).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'scale-in'.
components.exampleScenario: Hero icon pops in at center, bounces with spring physics, then text appears around it.

components.name: Vertical Tab Scene
useCases: Narrator walks through N topics sequentially (e.g. 3 health factors, 4 investment strategies)
Left-side tab list with progress indicator, right-side image/visual for active topic
Voiceover-synchronized topic switching with smooth transitions
components.whenToUse: When the narrator discusses 2-6 topics sequentially and each topic needs a visual. Tabs highlight in sync with voiceover timing. Great for listicle-style segments.
components.whenNotToUse: When comparing two entities head-to-head (use comparison-table or split-comparison). When showing data charts (use specific chart templates). When there's only one topic (use title-card).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.dataChart.type = 'vertical-tabs'. Provide items: [{id, title, description, image?}]. Optional: activeTimings (frame numbers), title, subtitle, showProgress, imagePosition.
components.exampleScenario: Scene: 'Top 3 factors affecting health' — tabs: Sleep, Nutrition, Exercise. As narrator discusses each, the tab highlights and right side shows relevant image.

components.name: Frosted Surface
useCases: Soft transparent card with vintage or warm feel
Background showing through with heavy blur and tint
Narrative or historical content containers
components.whenToUse: Container should have a soft, warm frosted-glass look — heavier blur and warmer tint than standard glass surface.
components.whenNotToUse: Clean modern glass look (use glass). Fully opaque cards (use flat). Attention-demanding cards (use glow).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.surface = 'frosted'.
components.exampleScenario: Historical quote card with frosted warm-tinted background over film-grain atmosphere.

components.name: Elevated Surface
useCases: Card with depth shadow giving floating appearance
Layered card stacks with visual depth
Important content cards that need to 'pop' from background
components.whenToUse: Container needs to feel like it's floating above the surface with shadow depth. Material design inspired elevation.
components.whenNotToUse: Transparent/glass look needed (use glass). Flat minimal style (use flat). Glowing emphasis (use glow).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.surface = 'elevated'.
components.exampleScenario: Data comparison cards stacked with progressive elevation — each card casts a shadow on the one below.

components.name: Tilt Card
useCases: 3D tilt showcase effect for cards or containers
Simulating mouse-hover depth on a card in video
Adding subtle 3D parallax to any child content
components.whenToUse: When a card or container should gently oscillate in 3D space for visual depth. Wrapper component — wrap any children. Uses Lissajous-pattern sine/cos for organic non-repeating tilt.
components.whenNotToUse: When content should stay flat and static (no tilt needed). When the scene is data-heavy and tilt would distract from readability.
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = 'tilt-card'. Optional: visual.motionConfig.maxTilt (default 8), visual.motionConfig.speed (default 1).
components.exampleScenario: Location card with map slowly tilts in 3D, giving a premium showcase feel.

components.name: Location Map Scene
useCases: Location reveal with animated SVG map drawing
City or country highlight with pin drop animation
Geographic context card with coordinates
components.whenToUse: When revealing a location with visual flair — SVG streets draw progressively, buildings fade in, pin drops with spring. Card starts collapsed and expands. Wrapped in TiltCard for 3D depth.
components.whenNotToUse: When showing multiple locations simultaneously (use comparison-table or split-comparison). When location is just a label without visual emphasis (use title-card).
components.storyboardHint: Set visual.dataChart.type = 'location-map'. Provide location ('Istanbul, Turkey'), coordinates ('41.0082° N, 28.9784° E'). Optional: pinColor, expandAtFrame.
components.exampleScenario: Scene reveals 'Istanbul, Turkey' — card expands, streets draw, buildings appear, pin drops, text fades in with green underline.

components.name: Retro Grid
useCases: Vaporwave/synthwave aesthetic backgrounds
Tech-focused scenes with perspective depth
Data explainer scenes needing a futuristic floor grid
components.whenToUse: Scene needs a futuristic, tech-forward background with perspective depth. Grid lines converge to vanishing point and scroll toward viewer.
components.whenNotToUse: Organic or cinematic scenes (use film-grain). Clean minimal backgrounds (use dot-grid). Scenes with busy foreground content that would clash with strong grid lines.
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = "retro-grid".
components.exampleScenario: Technology comparison scene with a perspective grid floor scrolling underneath flat data cards.

components.name: Shooting Stars
useCases: Space or cosmic themed scene backgrounds
Dramatic hero moments with ambient motion
Dark scenes needing subtle movement without distraction
components.whenToUse: Scene needs subtle ambient motion on a dark background. Stars streak diagonally at staggered intervals. Frame-deterministic positioning.
components.whenNotToUse: Bright or analytical scenes (use dot-grid). Scenes needing heavy texture (use film-grain). Already busy backgrounds.
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = "shooting-stars".
components.exampleScenario: Hero stat reveal with shooting stars streaking behind a glowing counter card on dark background.

components.name: Blurry Blob
useCases: Soft ambient colored backgrounds
Premium feel for key reveals and hero moments
Organic drifting color behind glass/frosted surfaces
components.whenToUse: Scene needs a soft, premium background with slowly drifting colored blobs. Works especially well behind glass surfaces. Uses palette accent colors.
components.whenNotToUse: Clean analytical scenes (use dot-grid). High-energy tech scenes (use retro-grid). Historical/vintage scenes (use film-grain).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.atmosphere = "blurry-blob".
components.exampleScenario: Glass surface card with stat floats over slowly drifting pink and blue blobs.

components.name: Text Shimmer
useCases: Title text with metallic shine sweep
Emphasis on key words or stats with glossy highlight
Premium branding text with looping shimmer
components.whenToUse: Text needs a premium metallic shine effect sweeping across it. Supports looping for continuous shimmer or single-pass for entrance.
components.whenNotToUse: Per-character animation needed (use stagger-text-reveal). Text cycling between values (use text-rotate). Subtle entrance only (use blur-fade-in).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = "text-shimmer".
components.exampleScenario: Hero title "GOLD STANDARD" with a metallic shimmer continuously sweeping left to right.

components.name: Glitch Text
useCases: Digital/tech aesthetic text with RGB channel split
Error or warning messages with glitch effect
Data corruption or system-themed scenes
components.whenToUse: Text should periodically glitch with RGB channel separation and jitter. Creates a digital/techy feel. Deterministic glitch timing from frame.
components.whenNotToUse: Clean professional text (use stagger-text-reveal). Smooth animations (use blur-fade-in). Continuous cycling text (use text-rotate).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = "glitch-text".
components.exampleScenario: Title "SYSTEM ERROR" periodically glitches with red/cyan channel split every 12 frames.

components.name: Typing Text
useCases: Typewriter-style text reveal with blinking cursor
Code or terminal output simulation
Sequential fact/stat revelation
components.whenToUse: Text should appear character by character like being typed, with a blinking cursor. Cursor disappears after text completes.
components.whenNotToUse: All text should appear at once (use blur-fade-in). Per-word dramatic entrance (use stagger-text-reveal). Text cycling (use text-rotate).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.motion = "typing-text".
components.exampleScenario: Terminal-style: "The global economy produces  trillion annually." types out character by character.

components.name: Neon Gradient Surface
useCases: Hero cards with animated neon border effect
Premium highlight cards that demand attention
Key stat or CTA cards with rotating gradient edge
components.whenToUse: Card needs maximum visual impact with a rotating neon gradient border. The conic gradient spins continuously creating a mesmerizing edge effect.
components.whenNotToUse: Subtle or secondary content (use glass or flat). Multiple cards in a grid (too many neon borders = visual noise). Clean data visualization (use flat).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.surface = "neon-gradient".
components.exampleScenario: Hero stat card showing key revenue figure with rotating pink-to-blue neon gradient border.

components.name: Backlight Surface
useCases: Cards with colored spotlight glow behind them
Emphasis cards that float above the background
Premium content containers with depth illusion
components.whenToUse: Card should appear illuminated from behind with a large colored glow that extends beyond card edges. Glow pulses gently and drifts for organic feel.
components.whenNotToUse: Fully opaque cards without effects (use flat). Transparent glass look (use glass). Border-focused glow (use glow or neon-gradient).
components.storyboardHint: In scene detail, set: visual.surface = "backlight".
components.exampleScenario: Data card with key metric floats with a soft blue backlight glow radiating from behind it.
